content.text: All 17 green. Now I need a CLI entry script for the workflow, the workflow itself, and the act test harness.

input.content:
# Thin CLI wrapper. Pwsh script-level params must live at the top of a file,
# so this exists as a separate entrypoint that dot-sources the library.
[CmdletBinding()]
param(
    [Parameter(Mandatory)] [string] $RulesPath,
    [Parameter(Mandatory)] [string] $FilesPath
)

. "$PSScriptRoot/PrLabelAssigner.ps1"
Invoke-PrLabelAssigner -RulesPath $RulesPath -FilesPath $FilesPath

content.text: Now the workflow. I'll have it read fixture files committed to the repo (passed via test harness) and emit labels.

input.content:
name: PR Label Assigner

on:
  push:
  pull_request:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      rules_path:
        description: Path to rules JSON file
        required: false
        default: rules.json
      files_path:
        description: Path to changed-files list (one path per line)
        required: false
        default: fixtures/files.txt

permissions:
  contents: read

env:
  RULES_PATH: ${{ github.event.inputs.rules_path || 'rules.json' }}
  FILES_PATH: ${{ github.event.inputs.files_path || 'fixtures/files.txt' }}

jobs:
  test:
    name: Pester unit tests
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Run Pester tests
        shell: pwsh
        run: |
          $cfg = New-PesterConfiguration
          $cfg.Run.Path = './PrLabelAssigner.Tests.ps1'
          $cfg.Run.Exit = $true
          $cfg.Output.Verbosity = 'Detailed'
          Invoke-Pester -Configuration $cfg

  assign-labels:
    name: Assign labels
    needs: test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Show fixture
        shell: pwsh
        run: |
          Write-Host "Rules file: $env:RULES_PATH"
          Write-Host "Files list: $env:FILES_PATH"
          Get-Content -LiteralPath $env:FILES_PATH | ForEach-Object { Write-Host "  changed: $_" }
      - name: Run label assigner
        id: assign
        shell: pwsh
        run: |
          $labels = & ./Invoke-PrLabelAssigner.ps1 -RulesPath $env:RULES_PATH -FilesPath $env:FILES_PATH
          if (-not $labels) { $labels = @() }
          $joined = ($labels -join ',')
          Write-Host "LABELS_BEGIN"
          foreach ($l in $labels) { Write-Host "label: $l" }
          Write-Host "LABELS_END"
          Write-Host "labels_csv=$joined"

  FILES_PATH: ${{ github.event.inputs.files_path || 'fixtures/files.txt' }}

jobs:

    steps:

      - name: Run Pester tests

        run: |

          Write-Host "Files list: $env:FILES_PATH"

          Write-Host "LABELS_BEGIN"
          foreach ($l in $labels) { Write-Host "label: $l" }
          Write-Host "LABELS_END"
          Write-Host "labels_csv=$joined"

content.text:
Done. Summary:

**Files created:**
- `PrLabelAssigner.ps1` — library: `Test-GlobMatch`, `Get-AssignedLabels`, `Invoke-PrLabelAssigner` (glob → regex translator supporting `*`, `**`, `?`)
- `Invoke-PrLabelAssigner.ps1` — thin CLI wrapper for the workflow
- `PrLabelAssigner.Tests.ps1` — 17 Pester tests built TDD-style (red → green)
- `rules.json` — default rules with labels and priorities
- `fixtures/files.txt` — fixture used by the workflow
- `.github/workflows/pr-label-assigner.yml` — `push` / `pull_request` / `workflow_dispatch`, two jobs (`test` then `assign-labels`), uses `actions/checkout@v4` and `shell: pwsh`
- `Run-ActTests.ps1` — workflow-structure + actionlint + 3-case act harness with exact-label assertions
- `act-result.txt` — 247-line artifact, all 3 cases passed

**Results:**
- Pester: 17/17 pass
- actionlint: clean
- act (3 cases through the real workflow):
  - `docs-only` → `[documentation]` ✓
  - `mixed-priorities` → `[tests,api,backend,documentation,dependencies]` (exact priority order) ✓
  - `no-matches` → `[]` ✓
  - Both jobs (`Pester unit tests`, `Assign labels`) reported `Job succeeded` for every case
